steps.title: Undo the square root
steps.content: Start with $$r=\sqrt{6w-4}$$ Square both sides to remove the radical: $$r^2=6w-4$$

steps.title: Solve for w
steps.content: Add $4$ to both sides, then divide by $6$: $$r^2+4=6w$$ $$w=\frac{r^2+4}{6}=\frac{r^2}{6}+\frac{2}{3}$$

steps.title: Use the condition on w
steps.content: The condition $w\gt \frac{2}{3}$ makes $6w-4$ positive, so squaring both sides is valid for the original square-root equation.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: The expression $w=\frac{r^2}{6}+\frac{2}{3}$ matches the correct answer, C.
